channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Quarter-Metal Superconductivity in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang

We investigate the recently discovered multiple superconducting states in rhombohedral graphene quarter metal. We demonstrate that one of these states features a single-spin, single-valley, single-band, single-Fermi-pocket parent state and is most likely a chiral topological pair-density wave, chara…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026603] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang

We investigate the recently discovered multiple superconducting states in rhombohedral graphene quarter metal. We demonstrate that one of these states features a single-spin, single-valley, single-band, single-Fermi-pocket parent state and is most likely a chiral topological pair-density wave, chara…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026603] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quarter-Metal Superconductivity in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.dc:creator: Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Supersymmetric Landau Levels in Subwavelength Type-I Dirac Metasurfaces
item.description:
Author(s): Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou

Dirac points, arising from intrinsic space-group symmetry in hexagonal lattices, have been extensively explored in graphene and photonic crystals. However, their characteristic scales, confined either to atomic level ($∼Å$) or limited by the operational wavelength ($∼λ$), pose fundamental challenges…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 023802] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou

Dirac points, arising from intrinsic space-group symmetry in hexagonal lattices, have been extensively explored in graphene and photonic crystals. However, their characteristic scales, confined either to atomic level (∼Å) or limited by the operational wavelength (∼λ), pose fundamental challenges for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 023802] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Supersymmetric Landau Levels in Subwavelength Type-I Dirac Metasurfaces
item.dc:creator: Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Shapiro Resonances in Mechanically Modulated Exciton-Polariton Josephson Junctions
item.description:
Author(s): I. Carraro-Haddad, D. L. Chafatinos, A. A. Reynoso, A. E. Bruchhausen, A. S. Kuznetsov, K. Biermann, P. V. Santos, G. Usaj, and A. Fainstein

We experimentally investigate the dynamics of Josephson junctions between two confined exciton-polariton condensates when periodically modulated via self-induced GHz mechanical oscillations. We show that the energy detuning between the condensates displays a plateau behavior akin to the Shapiro step…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026901] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Carraro-Haddad, D. L. Chafatinos, A. A. Reynoso, A. E. Bruchhausen, A. S. Kuznetsov, K. Biermann, P. V. Santos, G. Usaj, and A. Fainstein

We experimentally investigate the dynamics of Josephson junctions between two confined exciton-polariton condensates when periodically modulated via self-induced GHz mechanical oscillations. We show that the energy detuning between the condensates displays a plateau behavior akin to the Shapiro step…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026901] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Shapiro Resonances in Mechanically Modulated Exciton-Polariton Josephson Junctions
item.dc:creator: I. Carraro-Haddad, D. L. Chafatinos, A. A. Reynoso, A. E. Bruchhausen, A. S. Kuznetsov, K. Biermann, P. V. Santos, G. Usaj, and A. Fainstein

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Microscopic Evidence of Spin-Driven Multiferroicity and Topological Spin Textures in Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiI}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

In type-II multiferroics, noncollinear spin textures are expected to induce electric polarization directly, leading to strong magnetoelectric coupling. Realizing such spin-driven multiferroicity in two-dimensional (2D) systems, and elucidating the interplay between local spins and electric polarizat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026402] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

In type-II multiferroics, noncollinear spin textures are expected to induce electric polarization directly, leading to strong magnetoelectric coupling. Realizing such spin-driven multiferroicity in two-dimensional (2D) systems, and elucidating the interplay between local spins and electric polarizat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026402] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic Evidence of Spin-Driven Multiferroicity and Topological Spin Textures in Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiI}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: In-Plane Anomalous Features in the 3D Quantum Hall Regime
item.description:
Author(s): Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang

Studies of the 3D quantum Hall effect (QHE) have primarily emphasized transport features that mimic the well-established 2D QHE. In this Letter, we show that qualitatively new features arise when an in-plane magnetic field is applied to a 3D Weyl semimetal in the quantum Hall regime. An unexpected H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026602] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang

Studies of the 3D quantum Hall effect (QHE) have primarily emphasized transport features that mimic the well-established 2D QHE. In this Letter, we show that qualitatively new features arise when an in-plane magnetic field is applied to a 3D Weyl semimetal in the quantum Hall regime. An unexpected H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026602] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: In-Plane Anomalous Features in the 3D Quantum Hall Regime
item.dc:creator: Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ferromagnetic Interface Engineering of Spin-Charge Conversion in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou

Spin-orbit torque efficiency is conventionally fixed by bulk materials. $D$-wave altermagnets introduce an additional nonrelativistic spin-charge conversion channel beyond the inverse spin-Hall effect. Using prototypical candidate ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$ as an example, we show that the adjacent ferroma…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026702] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou

Spin-orbit torque efficiency is conventionally fixed by bulk materials. D-wave altermagnets introduce an additional nonrelativistic spin-charge conversion channel beyond the inverse spin-Hall effect. Using prototypical candidate RuO2 as an example, we show that the adjacent ferromagnet alone can dic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026702] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Ferromagnetic Interface Engineering of Spin-Charge Conversion in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Rigid-Body Anisotropy in Noncollinear Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Zheng Liu, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

In Mn3Sn, spin-orbit coupling is linked to both the Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction and biaxial single-ion anisotropy.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026703] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zheng Liu, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

In Mn3Sn, spin-orbit coupling is linked to both the Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction and biaxial single-ion anisotropy.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026703] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Rigid-Body Anisotropy in Noncollinear Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Zheng Liu, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Atomistic Structure of Transient Switching States in Ferroelectric AlScN
item.description:
Author(s): Jiawei Huang, Jinyang Li, Xinyue Guo, Tongqi Wen, David J. Srolovitz, Zhen Chen, Zuhuang Chen, and Shi Liu

We provide atomistic insights into the microscopic mechanism of polarization switching in wurtzite ferroelectric AlScN by integrating advanced thin-film fabrication, ferroelectric switching dynamics characterizations, high-resolution scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM), and large-scale …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026801] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiawei Huang, Jinyang Li, Xinyue Guo, Tongqi Wen, David J. Srolovitz, Zhen Chen, Zuhuang Chen, and Shi Liu

We provide atomistic insights into the microscopic mechanism of polarization switching in wurtzite ferroelectric AlScN by integrating advanced thin-film fabrication, ferroelectric switching dynamics characterizations, high-resolution scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM), and large-scale …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026801] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Atomistic Structure of Transient Switching States in Ferroelectric AlScN
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Huang, Jinyang Li, Xinyue Guo, Tongqi Wen, David J. Srolovitz, Zhen Chen, Zuhuang Chen, and Shi Liu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spontaneous Twirls and Structural Frustration in Moiré Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

Structural twirls form spontaneously in the domain wall networks of some moiré materials. We show that in heterobilayers, neighboring twirl chiralities tend to antialign, forming staggered patterns that are well described by antiferromagnetic lattice ${ϕ}^{4}$ theories. In moiré systems with triangu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026101] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

Structural twirls form spontaneously in the domain wall networks of some moiré materials. We show that in heterobilayers, neighboring twirl chiralities tend to antialign, forming staggered patterns that are well described by antiferromagnetic lattice ϕ4 theories. In moiré systems with triangular dom…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026101] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Spontaneous Twirls and Structural Frustration in Moiré Materials
item.dc:creator: Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: One-Dimensional ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ Topological Skin Effect Driven by Acoustic Lossy Couplings
item.description:
Author(s): Shuochen Wang, Wei Xiong, Zhiwang Zhang, Ying Cheng, and Xiaojun Liu

Recently, the non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) has attracted significant interest in condensed-matter physics due to its distinctive phenomenon of the bulk states’ localization at boundaries. With the establishment of non-Bloch framework, the NHSE can be characterized accurately using the generalize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026601] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuochen Wang, Wei Xiong, Zhiwang Zhang, Ying Cheng, and Xiaojun Liu

Recently, the non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) has attracted significant interest in condensed-matter physics due to its distinctive phenomenon of the bulk states’ localization at boundaries. With the establishment of non-Bloch framework, the NHSE can be characterized accurately using the generalize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026601] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: One-Dimensional ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ Topological Skin Effect Driven by Acoustic Lossy Couplings
item.dc:creator: Shuochen Wang, Wei Xiong, Zhiwang Zhang, Ying Cheng, and Xiaojun Liu

item.title: Altermagnet-Driven Magnon Spin Splitting Nernst Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

Magnonic spin current generation in antiferromagnets has emerged as an important topic in spintronics, where a strong external magnetic field or a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) is generally required. Recently, a type of antiferromagnets characterized by momentum-dependent nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026701] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

Magnonic spin current generation in antiferromagnets has emerged as an important topic in spintronics, where a strong external magnetic field or a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) is generally required. Recently, a type of antiferromagnets characterized by momentum-dependent nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026701] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnet-Driven Magnon Spin Splitting Nernst Effect
item.dc:creator: Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Inferring Charge-Noise Source Locations from Correlations in Spin Qubits
item.description:
Author(s): J. S. Rojas-Arias, A. Noiri, J. Yoneda, P. Stano, T. Nakajima, K. Takeda, T. Kobayashi, G. Scappucci, S. Tarucha, and D. Loss

We investigate low-frequency noise in a spin-qubit device made in isotopically purified Si/Si-Ge. Observing sizable cross-correlations among energy fluctuations of different qubits, we conclude that these fluctuations are dominated by charge noise. At low frequencies, the noise spectra are not well …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 027001] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. S. Rojas-Arias, A. Noiri, J. Yoneda, P. Stano, T. Nakajima, K. Takeda, T. Kobayashi, G. Scappucci, S. Tarucha, and D. Loss

We investigate low-frequency noise in a spin-qubit device made in isotopically purified Si/Si-Ge. Observing sizable cross-correlations among energy fluctuations of different qubits, we conclude that these fluctuations are dominated by charge noise. At low frequencies, the noise spectra are not well …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 027001] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Inferring Charge-Noise Source Locations from Correlations in Spin Qubits
item.dc:creator: J. S. Rojas-Arias, A. Noiri, J. Yoneda, P. Stano, T. Nakajima, K. Takeda, T. Kobayashi, G. Scappucci, S. Tarucha, and D. Loss

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ising Superconductivity in Noncentrosymmetric Bulk ${\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Dominik Volavka, Jozef Kačmarčík, Timon Moško, Zuzana Pribulová, Branislav Stropkai, Jozef Bednarčík, Yingzheng Gao, Owen Moulding, Marie-Aude Méasson, Christophe Marcenat, Thierry Klein, Shunsuke Sasaki, Laurent Cario, Martin Gmitra, Peter Samuely, and Tomas Samuely

Ising superconductivity allows in-plane upper critical magnetic fields to vastly surpass Pauli limit by locking the antiparallel electron spins of Cooper pairs in the out-of-plane direction. It was first explicitly demonstrated in fully two-dimensional monolayers of transition metal dichalcogenides …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016002] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dominik Volavka, Jozef Kačmarčík, Timon Moško, Zuzana Pribulová, Branislav Stropkai, Jozef Bednarčík, Yingzheng Gao, Owen Moulding, Marie-Aude Méasson, Christophe Marcenat, Thierry Klein, Shunsuke Sasaki, Laurent Cario, Martin Gmitra, Peter Samuely, and Tomas Samuely

Ising superconductivity allows in-plane upper critical magnetic fields to vastly surpass Pauli limit by locking the antiparallel electron spins of Cooper pairs in the out-of-plane direction. It was first explicitly demonstrated in fully two-dimensional monolayers of transition metal dichalcogenides …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016002] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Ising Superconductivity in Noncentrosymmetric Bulk ${\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Dominik Volavka, Jozef Kačmarčík, Timon Moško, Zuzana Pribulová, Branislav Stropkai, Jozef Bednarčík, Yingzheng Gao, Owen Moulding, Marie-Aude Méasson, Christophe Marcenat, Thierry Klein, Shunsuke Sasaki, Laurent Cario, Martin Gmitra, Peter Samuely, and Tomas Samuely

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Smaller Grains Are Not Stronger: Microcrystalline Metals at Ultrahigh Strain Rates
item.description:
Author(s): Laura Wu, Yuan Yao, Luyan Li, Qi Tang, and Mostafa Hassani

A textbook rule for the relationship between the structure and strength of a material breaks down for high-speed deformations, like those caused by strong impacts.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016104] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Laura Wu, Yuan Yao, Luyan Li, Qi Tang, and Mostafa Hassani

A textbook rule for the relationship between the structure and strength of a material breaks down for high-speed deformations, like those caused by strong impacts.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016104] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Smaller Grains Are Not Stronger: Microcrystalline Metals at Ultrahigh Strain Rates
item.dc:creator: Laura Wu, Yuan Yao, Luyan Li, Qi Tang, and Mostafa Hassani
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: X-Ray-Induced Quenching of the $^{229}\mathrm{Th}$ Clock Isomer in ${\mathrm{CaF}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Ming Guan et al.

Recent studies have shown that the lifetime of the isomeric $^{229}\mathrm{Th}$ doped in crystals can be shortened by x-ray or laser irradiation, a phenomenon referred to as isomer quenching. We investigate the temperature dependence of x-ray-induced quenching in $^{229}\mathrm{Th}:{\text{CaF}}_{2}$…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 013203] Published Thu Jan 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ming Guan et al.

Recent studies have shown that the lifetime of the isomeric Th229 doped in crystals can be shortened by x-ray or laser irradiation, a phenomenon referred to as isomer quenching. We investigate the temperature dependence of x-ray-induced quenching in Th229:CaF2 and identify a correlation with the aft…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 013203] Published Thu Jan 08, 2026
item.dc:title: X-Ray-Induced Quenching of the $^{229}\mathrm{Th}$ Clock Isomer in ${\mathrm{CaF}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Ming Guan et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Physical Spin Torques from Exactly Constrained Exchange-Correlation Torques
item.description:
Author(s): Jacques K. Desmarais, Kamel Bencheikh, Giovanni Vignale, and Stefano Pittalis

The problem of capturing physical spin torques in noncollinear magnetic systems has dominated the scene of spin-density functional theory (SDFT) in the last two decades. Progress has been hindered by the fact that the spin torque is directly connected to the divergence of the spin current, a quantit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016403] Published Wed Jan 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jacques K. Desmarais, Kamel Bencheikh, Giovanni Vignale, and Stefano Pittalis

The problem of capturing physical spin torques in noncollinear magnetic systems has dominated the scene of spin-density functional theory (SDFT) in the last two decades. Progress has been hindered by the fact that the spin torque is directly connected to the divergence of the spin current, a quantit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016403] Published Wed Jan 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Physical Spin Torques from Exactly Constrained Exchange-Correlation Torques
item.dc:creator: Jacques K. Desmarais, Kamel Bencheikh, Giovanni Vignale, and Stefano Pittalis
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: From Triangular Correlated Paramagnet to Multi-$q$ Noncoplanar Spin State in Spinel ${\mathrm{GeFe}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): L. Chaix, J. Robert, E. Chan, E. Ressouche, S. Petit, C. V. Colin, R. Ballou, J. Ollivier, L.-P. Regnault, E. Lhotel, V. Cathelin, S. Lenne, C. Cavenel, F. Damay, E. Suard, P. Strobel, C. Darie, S. deBrion, and V. Simonet

The spin arrangement in the magnetic frustrated spinel GeFe2O4 is stabilized in two steps, first as a triangular correlated paramagnet emerging from the pyrochlore lattice that finally orders as a rare 6-q magnetic structure.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016703] Published Wed Jan 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): L. Chaix, J. Robert, E. Chan, E. Ressouche, S. Petit, C. V. Colin, R. Ballou, J. Ollivier, L.-P. Regnault, E. Lhotel, V. Cathelin, S. Lenne, C. Cavenel, F. Damay, E. Suard, P. Strobel, C. Darie, S. deBrion, and V. Simonet

The spin arrangement in the magnetic frustrated spinel GeFe2O4 is stabilized in two steps, first as a triangular correlated paramagnet emerging from the pyrochlore lattice that finally orders as a rare 6-q magnetic structure.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016703] Published Wed Jan 07, 2026
item.dc:title: From Triangular Correlated Paramagnet to Multi-$q$ Noncoplanar Spin State in Spinel ${\mathrm{GeFe}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$
item.dc:creator: L. Chaix, J. Robert, E. Chan, E. Ressouche, S. Petit, C. V. Colin, R. Ballou, J. Ollivier, L.-P. Regnault, E. Lhotel, V. Cathelin, S. Lenne, C. Cavenel, F. Damay, E. Suard, P. Strobel, C. Darie, S. deBrion, and V. Simonet
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Hybrid Quantum Surface Acoustic Wave with Skyrmion Qubit for Quantum Information Processing
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Yuan Chen, Zhihui Peng, and Yu-xi Liu

Surface acoustic wave (SAW) devices are key components of classical communication systems and recently studied for quantum information processing. We here propose and study a hybrid quantum system composed of skyrmion qubit and a SAW cavity, which supports multiple long-lived phononic modes. The sys…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 013801] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Yuan Chen, Zhihui Peng, and Yu-xi Liu

Surface acoustic wave (SAW) devices are key components of classical communication systems and recently studied for quantum information processing. We here propose and study a hybrid quantum system composed of skyrmion qubit and a SAW cavity, which supports multiple long-lived phononic modes. The sys…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 013801] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Hybrid Quantum Surface Acoustic Wave with Skyrmion Qubit for Quantum Information Processing
item.dc:creator: Yu-Yuan Chen, Zhihui Peng, and Yu-xi Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Formation of Voids or Stacking-Fault Tetrahedra Induced by Local Chemical Variations in Face-Centered-Cubic Complex Concentrated Alloys
item.description:
Author(s): Yeping Lin, Chenyang Lu, Tengfei Yang, Zhengxiong Su, Yixin Deng, Wangyu Hu, Huiqiu Deng, Guanghong Lu, and Fei Gao

Understanding how elemental variations influence defect cluster formation is a longstanding challenge in materials science. By combining defect rates-based long-time dynamics with molecular dynamics and irradiation experiments, we identify a distinct, cluster-mediated mechanism—governed by element-s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016102] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yeping Lin, Chenyang Lu, Tengfei Yang, Zhengxiong Su, Yixin Deng, Wangyu Hu, Huiqiu Deng, Guanghong Lu, and Fei Gao

Understanding how elemental variations influence defect cluster formation is a longstanding challenge in materials science. By combining defect rates-based long-time dynamics with molecular dynamics and irradiation experiments, we identify a distinct, cluster-mediated mechanism—governed by element-s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016102] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Formation of Voids or Stacking-Fault Tetrahedra Induced by Local Chemical Variations in Face-Centered-Cubic Complex Concentrated Alloys
item.dc:creator: Yeping Lin, Chenyang Lu, Tengfei Yang, Zhengxiong Su, Yixin Deng, Wangyu Hu, Huiqiu Deng, Guanghong Lu, and Fei Gao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spatial Correlations of Charge Density Wave Order across the Transition in $2\mathrm{H}\text{−}{\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Seokjo Hong, Jaewhan Oh, Jemin Park, Woohyun Cho, Soyoung Lee, Colin Ophus, Yeongkwan Kim, Heejun Yang, SungBin Lee, and Yongsoo Yang

Charge density waves (CDWs) involve coupled amplitude and phase degrees of freedom, but direct access to local amplitude correlations remains experimentally challenging. Here, we report cryogenic four-dimensional scanning transmission electron microscopy measurements of CDW ordering in a $2\mathrm{H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016504] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Seokjo Hong, Jaewhan Oh, Jemin Park, Woohyun Cho, Soyoung Lee, Colin Ophus, Yeongkwan Kim, Heejun Yang, SungBin Lee, and Yongsoo Yang

Charge density waves (CDWs) involve coupled amplitude and phase degrees of freedom, but direct access to local amplitude correlations remains experimentally challenging. Here, we report cryogenic four-dimensional scanning transmission electron microscopy measurements of CDW ordering in a 2H−NbSe2 fl…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016504] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Spatial Correlations of Charge Density Wave Order across the Transition in $2\mathrm{H}\text{−}{\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Seokjo Hong, Jaewhan Oh, Jemin Park, Woohyun Cho, Soyoung Lee, Colin Ophus, Yeongkwan Kim, Heejun Yang, SungBin Lee, and Yongsoo Yang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Lindbladian versus Postselected non-Hermitian Topology
item.description:
Author(s): Alexandre Chaduteau, Derek K. K. Lee, and Frank Schindler

The recent topological classification of non-Hermitian “Hamiltonians” is usually interpreted in terms of pure quantum states that decay or grow with time. However, many-body systems with loss and gain are typically better described by mixed-state open quantum dynamics, which only correspond to pure-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016603] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alexandre Chaduteau, Derek K. K. Lee, and Frank Schindler

The recent topological classification of non-Hermitian “Hamiltonians” is usually interpreted in terms of pure quantum states that decay or grow with time. However, many-body systems with loss and gain are typically better described by mixed-state open quantum dynamics, which only correspond to pure-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016603] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Lindbladian versus Postselected non-Hermitian Topology
item.dc:creator: Alexandre Chaduteau, Derek K. K. Lee, and Frank Schindler
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetoelectric Torque in Polar Magnetic Bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Zhong Shen, Jun Chen, Xiaoyan Yao, and Shuai Dong

Energy-efficient fast switching of spin orientations or textures is a core issue of spintronics, which is highly demanded but remains challenging. Different from the mainstream routes based on spin-transfer torque or spin-orbit torque, here we propose another mechanism coined as magnetoelectric torq…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016702] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhong Shen, Jun Chen, Xiaoyan Yao, and Shuai Dong

Energy-efficient fast switching of spin orientations or textures is a core issue of spintronics, which is highly demanded but remains challenging. Different from the mainstream routes based on spin-transfer torque or spin-orbit torque, here we propose another mechanism coined as magnetoelectric torq…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016702] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetoelectric Torque in Polar Magnetic Bilayers
item.dc:creator: Zhong Shen, Jun Chen, Xiaoyan Yao, and Shuai Dong
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Tracking the Photoinduced Dynamics of a Dark Excitonic State in Single-Layer ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}$ via Resonant Autler-Townes Splitting
item.description:
Author(s): Angela Montanaro, Francesco Valiera, Francesca Giusti, Francesca Fassioli, Chiara Trovatello, Giacomo Jarc, Enrico Maria Rigoni, Fang Liu, Xiaoyang Zhu, Stefano Dal Conte, Giulio Cerullo, Martin Eckstein, and Daniele Fausti

A new three-pulse Autler-Townes technique unveils the ultrafast dynamics of a dark 2p exciton in monolayer WS2, revealing symmetry-dependent many-body screening previously hidden from optical probes.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016902] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Angela Montanaro, Francesco Valiera, Francesca Giusti, Francesca Fassioli, Chiara Trovatello, Giacomo Jarc, Enrico Maria Rigoni, Fang Liu, Xiaoyang Zhu, Stefano Dal Conte, Giulio Cerullo, Martin Eckstein, and Daniele Fausti

A new three-pulse Autler-Townes technique unveils the ultrafast dynamics of a dark 2p exciton in monolayer WS2, revealing symmetry-dependent many-body screening previously hidden from optical probes.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016902] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Tracking the Photoinduced Dynamics of a Dark Excitonic State in Single-Layer ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}$ via Resonant Autler-Townes Splitting
item.dc:creator: Angela Montanaro, Francesco Valiera, Francesca Giusti, Francesca Fassioli, Chiara Trovatello, Giacomo Jarc, Enrico Maria Rigoni, Fang Liu, Xiaoyang Zhu, Stefano Dal Conte, Giulio Cerullo, Martin Eckstein, and Daniele Fausti

item.title: Complex Phonon Behaviors Dictate Anisotropic and Nonmonotonic Thermal Transport in Ice Polymorphs
item.description:
Author(s): Rong Qiu, Qiyu Zeng, Bo Chen, Jinsen Han, Jiahao Chen, Qunchao Tong, Kaiguo Chen, Dongdong Kang, Xiaoxiang Yu, Han Wang, and Jiayu Dai

The thermal conductivity of ice polymorphs constitutes a critical parameter in multidisciplinary research spanning cryobiology, atmospheric physics, and planetary science. However, the intricate structures and phonon dynamics pose significant challenges to understanding thermal transport in ice poly…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016301] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rong Qiu, Qiyu Zeng, Bo Chen, Jinsen Han, Jiahao Chen, Qunchao Tong, Kaiguo Chen, Dongdong Kang, Xiaoxiang Yu, Han Wang, and Jiayu Dai

The thermal conductivity of ice polymorphs constitutes a critical parameter in multidisciplinary research spanning cryobiology, atmospheric physics, and planetary science. However, the intricate structures and phonon dynamics pose significant challenges to understanding thermal transport in ice poly…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016301] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Large Spontaneous Nonreciprocal Charge Transport in a Zero-Magnetization Antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Kenta Sudo, Yuki Yanagi, Mitsuru Akaki, Hiroshi Tanida, and Motoi Kimata

An antiferromagnet with a zigzag magnetic structure exhibits a diode effect that has potential applications in spintronics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016503] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kenta Sudo, Yuki Yanagi, Mitsuru Akaki, Hiroshi Tanida, and Motoi Kimata

An antiferromagnet with a zigzag magnetic structure exhibits a diode effect that has potential applications in spintronics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016503] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Large Spontaneous Nonreciprocal Charge Transport in a Zero-Magnetization Antiferromagnet

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Odd-Chern-Number Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect at Even Filling in Moire Rhombohedral Heptalayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Qianling Liu, Zhiyu Wang, Xiangyan Han, Zhuoxian Li, Bohao Li, Sicheng Zhou, Lihong Hu, Zhuangzhuang Qu, Chunrui Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Zheng Vitto Han, Bingbing Tong, Guangtong Liu, Li Lu, Fengcheng Wu, and Jianming Lu

The quantum anomalous Hall effect at even filling has been observed in rhombohedral heptalayer graphene moiré superlattices – previous observations were for odd-integer filling factors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016602] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qianling Liu, Zhiyu Wang, Xiangyan Han, Zhuoxian Li, Bohao Li, Sicheng Zhou, Lihong Hu, Zhuangzhuang Qu, Chunrui Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Zheng Vitto Han, Bingbing Tong, Guangtong Liu, Li Lu, Fengcheng Wu, and Jianming Lu

The quantum anomalous Hall effect at even filling has been observed in rhombohedral heptalayer graphene moiré superlattices – previous observations were for odd-integer filling factors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016602] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Odd-Chern-Number Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect at Even Filling in Moire Rhombohedral Heptalayer Graphene

item.title: Common Sublattice-Pure Van Hove Singularities in the Kagome Superconductors $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K}$, Rb, Cs)
item.description:
Author(s): Yujie Lan, Yuhao Lei, Congcong Le, Brenden R. Ortiz, Nicholas C. Plumb, Milan Radovic, Xianxin Wu, Ming Shi, Stephen D. Wilson, and Yong Hu

Kagome materials offer a versatile platform for exploring correlated and topological quantum states, where Van Hove singularities (VHSs) play a pivotal role in driving electronic instabilities, exhibiting distinct behaviors depending on electron filling and interaction settings. In the recently disc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016401] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yujie Lan, Yuhao Lei, Congcong Le, Brenden R. Ortiz, Nicholas C. Plumb, Milan Radovic, Xianxin Wu, Ming Shi, Stephen D. Wilson, and Yong Hu

Kagome materials offer a versatile platform for exploring correlated and topological quantum states, where Van Hove singularities (VHSs) play a pivotal role in driving electronic instabilities, exhibiting distinct behaviors depending on electron filling and interaction settings. In the recently disc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016401] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Common Sublattice-Pure Van Hove Singularities in the Kagome Superconductors $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K}$, Rb, Cs)
item.dc:creator: Yujie Lan, Yuhao Lei, Congcong Le, Brenden R. Ortiz, Nicholas C. Plumb, Milan Radovic, Xianxin Wu, Ming Shi, Stephen D. Wilson, and Yong Hu

item.title: Stripe-Nematic Phase of Composite Fermions
item.description:
Author(s): Chengyu Wang, S. K. Singh, C. T. Tai, A. Gupta, L. N. Pfeiffer, K. W. Baldwin, and M. Shayegan

Electronic stripe-nematic phases are fascinating, strongly correlated states characterized by spontaneous rotational symmetry breaking. In the quantum Hall regime, such phases typically emerge at half-filled, high-orbital-index ($N≥2$) Landau levels (LLs) where the short-range Coulomb interaction is…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016501] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chengyu Wang, S. K. Singh, C. T. Tai, A. Gupta, L. N. Pfeiffer, K. W. Baldwin, and M. Shayegan

Electronic stripe-nematic phases are fascinating, strongly correlated states characterized by spontaneous rotational symmetry breaking. In the quantum Hall regime, such phases typically emerge at half-filled, high-orbital-index (N≥2) Landau levels (LLs) where the short-range Coulomb interaction is s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016501] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Stripe-Nematic Phase of Composite Fermions
item.dc:creator: Chengyu Wang, S. K. Singh, C. T. Tai, A. Gupta, L. N. Pfeiffer, K. W. Baldwin, and M. Shayegan

item.title: Orbital Inverse Faraday and Cotton-Mouton Effects in Hall Fluids
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Cardoso, Erlend Syljuåsen, and Alexander V. Balatsky

We report two light-induced orbital magnetization effects in quantum Hall (QH) fluids, stemming from their transverse response. The first is a purely transverse contribution to the inverse Faraday effect (IFE), where circularly polarized light induces a dc magnetization by stirring the charged fluid…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016502] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Cardoso, Erlend Syljuåsen, and Alexander V. Balatsky

We report two light-induced orbital magnetization effects in quantum Hall (QH) fluids, stemming from their transverse response. The first is a purely transverse contribution to the inverse Faraday effect (IFE), where circularly polarized light induces a dc magnetization by stirring the charged fluid…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016502] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital Inverse Faraday and Cotton-Mouton Effects in Hall Fluids
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Cardoso, Erlend Syljuåsen, and Alexander V. Balatsky

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Half-Quantized Chiral Edge Current in a $C=1/2$ Parity Anomaly State
item.description:
Author(s): Deyi Zhuo, Bomin Zhang, Humian Zhou, Han Tay, Xiaoda Liu, Zhiyuan Xi, Chui-Zhen Chen, and Cui-Zu Chang

A single massive Dirac surface band is predicted to exhibit a half-quantized Hall conductance, a hallmark of the $C=1/2$ parity anomaly state in quantum field theory. Experimental signatures of the $C=1/2$ parity anomaly state have been observed in semimagnetic topological insulator (TI) bilayers, y…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016601] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Deyi Zhuo, Bomin Zhang, Humian Zhou, Han Tay, Xiaoda Liu, Zhiyuan Xi, Chui-Zhen Chen, and Cui-Zu Chang

A single massive Dirac surface band is predicted to exhibit a half-quantized Hall conductance, a hallmark of the C=1/2 parity anomaly state in quantum field theory. Experimental signatures of the C=1/2 parity anomaly state have been observed in semimagnetic topological insulator (TI) bilayers, yet w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016601] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Half-Quantized Chiral Edge Current in a $C=1/2$ Parity Anomaly State
item.dc:creator: Deyi Zhuo, Bomin Zhang, Humian Zhou, Han Tay, Xiaoda Liu, Zhiyuan Xi, Chui-Zhen Chen, and Cui-Zu Chang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
